// DESCRIPTION: Fabric Activator alert rule for detecting adverse event safety signal spikes. Deploy this configuration in the Activator item within your Fabric workspace.

activator_rule.name: Drug Safety Signal Spike Alert
activator_rule.description: Triggers when a drug+reaction combination shows a serious adverse event count exceeding 2× the 30-day rolling average within the past 24 hours. Targets hospitalization and death outcomes for regulatory-critical escalation.

data_source.description: Connect Activator to the Eventhouse KQL database containing the AdverseEvents table and materialized views.

trigger.description: This KQL query runs on the configured schedule and returns rows that represent active safety signals. Each returned row triggers an alert action.
trigger.query:
// Detect safety signal spikes: 7-day count > 2× expected from 30-day baseline
let spike_multiplier = 2.0;
let min_event_count = 5;
let recent =
    AdverseEvents
    | where is_serious == true
    | where ingest_timestamp > ago(7d)
    | summarize
        recent_count = count(),
        recent_hosp  = countif(hospitalization),
        recent_death = countif(death),
        latest_event = max(ingest_timestamp)
      by generic_name, primary_reaction;
let baseline =
    AdverseEvents
    | where is_serious == true
    | where ingest_timestamp > ago(30d)
    | summarize baseline_count = count()
      by generic_name, primary_reaction;
recent
| join kind=inner baseline on generic_name, primary_reaction
| extend expected_7d = (todouble(baseline_count) / 30.0) * 7.0
| extend spike_ratio = iff(expected_7d > 0, todouble(recent_count) / expected_7d, 0.0)
| where recent_count >= min_event_count
| where spike_ratio >= spike_multiplier
| project
    generic_name,
    primary_reaction,
    recent_count,
    baseline_count,
    expected_7d = round(expected_7d, 1),
    spike_ratio = round(spike_ratio, 2),
    recent_death,
    recent_hosp,
    latest_event,
    severity = case(
        recent_death > 0, "CRITICAL",
        recent_hosp > 0,  "HIGH",
        "MEDIUM"
    )
| order by spike_ratio desc

evaluation_schedule.description: Evaluates every 60 minutes. Adjust to 15-minute intervals for higher sensitivity during active monitoring periods.

condition.description: Alert fires when the query returns one or more rows (i.e., at least one drug+reaction combination exceeds the spike threshold).

cooldown.description: Suppress duplicate alerts for the same signal within this window to prevent alert fatigue. In Activator, configure this under 'Snooze duration'.

actions.description: Sends a rich adaptive card to a Teams channel with signal details, severity, and a drill-down action button.

target.channel_description: Post to #drug-safety-alerts or equivalent PV operations channel.

body.text: **Recommended Action:** Review individual case narratives in Eventhouse and assess whether a regulatory filing (IND Safety Report / CIOMS) is required.

actions.title: 🔍 Review Case Narratives
actions.tooltip: Open drill-down query in Fabric Eventhouse

actions.title: 📊 Open Signal Dashboard
actions.tooltip: Open the Real-Time Signal Room Dashboard

actions.title: 📋 Log in Safety Database
actions.tooltip: Create a new signal entry in your PV safety database

actions.description: Sends an email notification to the PV safety team with signal summary.

email_template.subject: [${severity}] Drug Safety Signal: ${generic_name} — ${primary_reaction}
email_template.body_html:
⚠️ Safety Signal Detected

FieldValue
Drug${generic_name}
Reaction${primary_reaction}
Severity${severity}
7-Day Count${recent_count}
30-Day Baseline${baseline_count}
Spike Ratio${spike_ratio}×
Deaths${recent_death}
Hospitalizations${recent_hosp}

Next Steps: Review case narratives and determine if regulatory action is required.

Open Eventhouse | Open Dashboard

setup_instructions.step_1: In your Fabric workspace, create a new Activator item: New → Activator.
setup_instructions.step_2: Select 'Eventhouse' as the data source and connect to DrugSafetyDB.
setup_instructions.step_3: Choose 'Start monitoring' and paste the trigger KQL query from activator_rule.trigger.query above.
setup_instructions.step_4: Configure the evaluation schedule (default: every 60 minutes).
setup_instructions.step_5: Set the condition: 'When query returns rows' (row count > 0).
setup_instructions.step_6: Add Action 1: Teams — paste the Teams webhook URL and the adaptive card JSON template.
setup_instructions.step_7: Add Action 2: Email — configure recipients and paste the email template.
setup_instructions.step_9: Activate and test with: AdverseEvents | take 1 (verify data flows).
setup_instructions.step_10: For production: Adjust spike_multiplier, min_event_count, and schedule as needed.
